kv.memory.schc-libschc-evaluation-2026-0: SCHC libSCHC evaluation 2026-06-30: evaluated upstream imec-idlab/libschc at a01d7856243d3aa1242918e31649d8a5ee92872e. License is GPL-3.0 dual/commercial, compatible with LICHEN GPL C firmware. Upstream README says implementation is experimental and missing RFC 8724 features. It is plain C, not a Zephyr module, with static schc_config.h/rules/rule_config.h integration and example rule tables. Rough arm-zephyr-eabi -Os object size for example config: bit_operations 1399 text, compressor 10745 text + 268 bss, fragmenter 20015 text + 3527 bss, picocoap 3160 text, jsmn 1224 text, schc 5339 text + 40 data, rcs 4 text; total about 41.9 KiB text and 3.8 KiB BSS before Zephyr/linker GC. Current LICHEN schc.c rough object size with same compiler is 6240 text, 0 BSS. Existing LICHEN SCHC vectors pass 12/12. Recommendation: do not import/swap libSCHC for embedded nodes now; keep LICHEN profile implementation and use libSCHC only as a reference or possible future gateway/prototype dependency after a dedicated rule-table adapter and fragmentation fit study.
kv.memory.workflow-rule-every-small-code: Workflow rule: every small code-generation or fix Bead must be followed by two code-review passes before it is considered done. Add this explicitly to acceptance criteria for new codegen/fix Beads.
kv.memory.zephyr-builds-ec2: Zephyr Linux-only builds/tests should use AWS EC2 when they cannot run on local macOS. Whenever using EC2 for Zephyr work, attach and use the prepared EBS builder cache; do not rebuild Zephyr dependencies from scratch unless the cache is unavailable or invalid. Local AWS profile: AdministratorAccess-921772462201. Persistent single-AZ EBS builder cache: vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 in us-east-2a, mount at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. It contains Zephyr SDK 0.16.8, west workspace pinned to Zephyr v3.7.0, Python venv, modules, ccache, and helper scripts. Attach it to a us-east-2a builder, mount via /mnt/lichen-zephyr/scripts/mount-volume.sh, source /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh, then build. Use SSH and AWS API console output to watch boot/cloud-init. Keep temporary instances short-lived/tagged/terminated and detach the EBS volume cleanly when done.
